cards.front: What is the main purpose of a Check Sum in a data transmission?
cards.back: To detect errors in data transmission by verifying the received data against the original data

cards.front: What is the main advantage of using Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) in data transmission?
cards.back: A low computational overhead and minimal impact on data transmission speed

cards.front: How does Point-to-Point Protocol (PPP) use Check Sum?
cards.back: PPP uses Check Sum to verify the integrity of data packets and detect errors during transmission

cards.front: What is the main difference between a Check Sum and a Cyclic Code?
cards.back: A Check Sum is a simple error detection mechanism, while a Cyclic Code is a more complex error detection and correction mechanism

cards.front: What is the primary function of the Check Sum field in a data packet?
cards.back: To contain the Calculated Cyclic Redundancy Check (CC) value, which is calculated using a polynomial equation
